steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: "Correspondingly distorted" establishes the cause-effect relationship: carbon rises → audio distorts. The transition must express what logically follows from this distortion.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-and-effect: because carbon levels distort the audio, listeners can perceive those rising levels through the distortion. The blank needs a transition signaling that what follows is a consequence of the distortion.
  • Choice A — "by comparison,": signals a parallel comparison between two separate things, so fails — listeners perceiving the distortion is a result of it, not a parallel item. ✗
  • Choice B — "thus,": signals logical consequence, so fits — the distortion is the mechanism by which listeners can hear the carbon changes. ✓
  • Choice C — "for instance,": signals a specific example of a general claim, so fails — listening is not one example among many; it is the direct outcome. ✗
  • Choice D — "furthermore,": signals an additional separate point, so fails — the listening is not a new independent fact but the consequence of the distortion already described. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
